Compartment Exam - 2023

Code: 65/C/1 (set-1)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  3. Please check that this question paper contains 38 questions.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into five Sections - A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A, Questions no. 1 to 18 are multiple choice questions (MCQs) and questions number 19 and 20 are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B, Questions no. 21 to 25 are very short answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C, Questions no. 26 to 31 are short answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D, Questions no. 32 to 35 are long answer (LA) type questions carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E, Questions no. 36 to 38 are case study based questions carrying 4 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section B, 3 questions in Section C, 2 questions in Section D and 2 questions in Section E.
  9. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A

This section comprises multiple choice questions (MCQs) of 1 mark each.
1.

If A is a square matrix of order 3 and |A| = 6, then the value of |adj A| is:

a. 6
b. 36
c. 27
d. 216
2.

The value of {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac \pi 6}{/tex} sin 3x dx is:

a. {tex}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}{/tex}
b. {tex}-\frac 13{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac 13{/tex}
3.

If {tex}\vec a{/tex}, {tex}\vec b{/tex} and {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} are all unit vectors and {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex}, then the value of {tex}\theta{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\frac{2 \pi}{3}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{5 \pi}{6}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{ \pi}{6}{/tex}
4.

The projection of vector {tex}\hat {\text i}{/tex} on the vector {tex}\hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}+2 \hat{\text k}{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}}{/tex}
b. {tex}\sqrt 6{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{2}{\sqrt{6}}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{3}{\sqrt{6}}{/tex}
5.

A family has 2 children and the elder child is a girl. The probability that both children are girls is:

a. {tex}\frac 14{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac 18{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac 12{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac 34{/tex}
6.

The vector equation of a line which passes through the point (2, -4, 5) and is parallel to the line {tex}\frac{\text x+3}{3}=\frac{4-\text y}{2}=\frac{\text z+8}{6}{/tex} is:

a. {tex}\vec{r}=(-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}){/tex} + {tex}\lambda(3 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex}
b. {tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{k}){/tex} + {tex}\lambda(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex}
c. {tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{k}){/tex} + {tex}\lambda(3 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex}
d. {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}){/tex} + {tex}\lambda(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-6 \hat{k}){/tex}
7.

For which value of x, are the determinants {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 2 \mathrm{x} & -3 \\ 5 & \mathrm{x} \end{array}\right|{/tex} and {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} 10 & 1 \\ -3 & 2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} equal?

a. {tex}\pm 3{/tex}
b. -3
c. {tex}\pm 2{/tex}
d. 2
8.

The value of the cofactor of the element of the second row and third column in the matrix {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 4 & 3 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} is:

a. 5
b. -5
c. -11
d. 11
9.

The difference of the order and the degree of the differential equation {tex}\left(\frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}\right)^2+\left(\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right)^3+\text x^4{/tex} = 0 is:

a. 1
b. 2
c. -1
d. 0
10.

If matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -1 \\ -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and A2 = kA, then the value of k is: 

a. 1
b. -2
c. 2
d. -1
11.

{tex}\int \frac{\cos 2 \text x}{\sin ^2 \text x \cdot \cos ^2 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} is equal to

a. tan x - cot x + C
b. -cot x - tan x + C
c. cot x + tan x + C
d. tan x - cot x - C
12.

The integrating factor of the differential equation (3x2 + y) {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dy}}{/tex} = x is

a. {tex}\frac {1}{\text x}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac {1}{\text x^2}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac {2}{\text x}{/tex}
d. {tex}-\frac {1}{\text x}{/tex}
13.

The point which lies in the half-plane 2x + y - 4 {tex}\leq{/tex} 0 is:

a. (0, 8)
b. (1, 1)
c. (5, 5)
d. (2, 2)
14.

If (cos x)y = (cos y)x, then {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} is equal to:

a. {tex}\frac{\text y \tan \text x+\log (\cos \text y)}{\text x \tan \text y-\log (\cos \text x)}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{\text x \tan \text y+\log (\cos \text x)}{\text y \tan \text x+\log (\cos \text y)}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{\text y \tan \text x-\log (\cos \text y)}{\text x \tan \text y-\log (\cos \text x)}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{\text y \tan \text x+\log (\cos \text y)}{\text x \tan \text y+\log (\cos \text x)}{/tex}
15.

It is given that X {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}. Then matrix X is:

a. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
b. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 0 & -1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
c. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
d. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 1 & -1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
16.

If ABCD is a parallelogram and AC and BD are its diagonals, then {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BD}}{/tex} is:

a. {tex}2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{DA}}{/tex}
b. {tex}2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}{/tex}
c. {tex}2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}{/tex}
d. {tex}2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{BD}}{/tex}
17.

If x = a cos {tex}\theta{/tex} + b sin {tex}\theta{/tex}, y = a sin {tex}\theta{/tex} - b cos {tex}\theta{/tex}, then which one of the following is true?

a. {tex}\text y^2 \frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}-\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\text y{/tex} = 0
b. {tex}\text y^2 \frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}+\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\text y{/tex} = 0
c. {tex}\text y^2 \frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}+\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}-\text y{/tex} = 0
d. {tex}\text y^2 \frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}-\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}-\text y{/tex} = 0
18.

The corner points of the bounded feasible region of an LPP are O(0, 0), A(250, 0), B(200, 50) and C(0, 175). If the maximum value of the objective function Z = 2ax + by occurs at the points A(250, 0) and B(200, 50), then the relation between a and b is:

a. 2a = b
b. 2a = 3b
c. a = b
d. a = 2b

Questions number 19 and 20 are Assertion and Reason based questions carrying 1 mark each. Two statements are given, one labelled Assertion (A) and the other labelled Reason (R). Select the correct answer from the codes (a), (b), (c) and (d) as given below.
19.

Assertion (A): The principal value of cot-1 {tex}(\sqrt 3){/tex} is {tex}\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}.
Reason (R): Domain of cot-1 x is {tex}\mathbb R{/tex} - {-1, 1}.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
20.

Assertion (A): Quadrilateral formed by vertices A(0, 0, 0), B(3, 4, 5), C(8, 8, 8) and D(5, 4, 3) is a rhombus.
Reason (R): ABCD is a rhombus if AB = BC = CD = DA, AC {tex}\neq{/tex} BD.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.

SECTION B

This section comprises very short answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each.
21.

If three non-zero vectors are {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} such that {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}{/tex}, then show that {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{c}{/tex}.
22.(i)

Simplify: {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\cos \text x}{1-\sin \text x}\right){/tex}

OR
(ii)

Prove that the greatest integer function f: {tex}\mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}{/tex}, given by f(x) = [x], is neither one-one nor onto.
23.

Function f is defined as f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{ccc} 2 \text x+2, & \text { if } & \text x<2 \\ \text k, & \text { if } & \text x=2 \\ 3\text x, & \text { if } & \text x>2 \end{array}\right.{/tex}
Find the value of k for which the function f is continuous at x = 2.
24.

Find the intervals in which the function f(x) = x4 - 4x3 + 4x2 + 15, is strictly increasing.
25.(i)

If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} are three vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}|{/tex} = 7, {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex} = 24, {tex}|\vec{c}|{/tex} = 25 and {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{0}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}{/tex}.

OR
(ii)

If a line makes angles {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex} and {tex}\gamma{/tex} with x-axis, y-axis and z-axis respectively, then prove that sin2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\gamma{/tex} = 2.

SECTION C

This section comprises short answer (SA) type questions of 3 marks each.
26.(i)

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac \pi 2} \frac{\text x \sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex}

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_1^3{/tex}{|(x - 1)| + |(x - 2)|} dx
27.(i)

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {xy}}{\text x^2+\text y^2}{/tex}, given that y = 1 when x = 0.

OR
(ii)

Find the particular solution of the differential equation (1 + x2) {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} + 2xy = {tex}\frac{1}{1+\text x^2}{/tex}, given that y = 0 when x = 1.
28.(i)

Out of two bags, bag A contains 2 white and 3 red balls and bag B contains 4 white and 5 red balls. One ball is drawn at random from one of the bags and is found to be red. Find the probability that it was drawn from bag B.

OR
(ii)

Out of a group of 50 people, 20 always speak the truth. Two persons are selected at random from the group (without replacement). Find the probability distribution of number of selected persons who always speak the truth.
29.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\cos \theta}{\sqrt{3-3 \sin \theta-\cos ^2 \theta}} \text d \theta{/tex}
30.

Solve the following Linear Programming Problem graphically:
Minimise z = 3x + 8y
subject to the constraints
3x + 4y {tex}\geq{/tex} 8
5x + 2y {tex}\geq{/tex} 11
x {tex}\geq{/tex} 0, y {tex}\geq{/tex} 0
31.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{2 \text x^2+1}{\text x^2\left(\text x^2+4\right)} \text {dx}{/tex}

SECTION D

This section comprises long answer type questions (LA) of 5 marks each.
32.

If matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1 and hence solve the following system of linear equations:
3x + 2y + z = 2000
4x + y + 3z = 2500
x + y + z = 900
33.(i)

Show that the lines {tex} \frac { x + 1 } { 3 } = \frac { y + 3 } { 5 } = \frac { z + 5 } { 7 }{/tex} and {tex} \frac { x - 2 } { 1 } = \frac { y - 4 } { 3 } = \frac { z - 6 } { 5 }{/tex} intersect. Also, find their point of intersection.

OR
(ii)

Find the shortest distance between the pair of lines {tex}\frac{\text x-1}{2}=\frac{\text y+1}{3}{/tex} = z and {tex}\frac{\text x+1}{5}=\frac{\text y-2}{1}{/tex}; z = 2.
34.

Find the area of the triangle ABC bounded by the lines represented by the equations 5x - 2y - 10 = 0, x - y - 9 = 0 and 3x - 4y - 6 = 0, using integration method.
35.(i)

Show that the relation S in set {tex}\mathbb R{/tex} of real numbers defined by
S = {(a, b): a {tex}\leq{/tex} b3, a {tex}\in{/tex} {tex}\mathbb R{/tex}, b {tex}\in{/tex} {tex}\mathbb R{/tex}} is neither reflexive, nor symmetric, nor transitive.

OR
(ii)

Let R be the relation defined in the set A = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7} by R = {(a, b): both a and b are either odd or even}. Show that R is an equivalence relation. Hence, find the elements of equivalence class [1].

SECTION E

This section comprises 3 case study based questions of 4 marks each.

Case Study - 1
36.

In a group activity class, there are 10 students whose ages are 16, 17, 15, 14, 19, 17, 16, 19, 16 and 15 years. One student is selected at random such that each has equal chance of being chosen and age of the student is recorded.

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Find the probability that the age of the selected student is a composite number.

 1
(ii)

Let X be the age of the selected student. What can be the value of X?

 1
(iii)(a)

Find the probability distribution of random variable X and hence find the mean age.

 2

OR
(iii)(b)

A student was selected at random and his age was found to be greater than 15 years. Find the probability that his age is a prime number.

 2

Case Study - 2
37.

A housing society wants to commission a swimming pool for its residents. For this, they have to purchase a square piece of land and dig this to such a depth that its capacity is 250 cubic metres. Cost of land is ₹ 500 per square metre. The cost of digging increases with the depth and cost for the whole pool is ₹ 4000 (depth)2.

Suppose the side of the square plot is x metres and depth is h metres.

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Write cost C(h) as a function in terms of h.

 1
(ii)

Find critical point.

 1
(iii)(a)

Use second derivative test to find the value of h for which cost of constructing the pool is minimum. What is the minimum cost of construction of the pool?

 2

OR
(iii)(b)

Use first derivative test to find the depth of the pool so that cost of construction is minimum. Also, find relation between x and h for minimum cost.

 2

Case Study - 3
38.

In an agricultural institute, scientists do experiments with varieties of seeds to grow them in different environments to produce healthy plants and get more yield.
A scientist observed that a particular seed grew very fast after germination. He had recorded growth of plant since germination and he said that its growth can be defined by the function
f(x) = {tex}\frac 13{/tex}x3 - 4x2 + 15x + 2, 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} x {tex}\leq{/tex} 10
where x is the number of days the plant is exposed to sunlight.

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

What are the critical points of the function f(x)?

 2
(ii)

Using second derivative test, find the minimum value of the function.

 2

27.(i)

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {xy}}{\text x^2+\text y^2}{/tex}, given that y = 1 when x = 0.

Solution:

{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {xy}}{\text x^2+\text y^2}{/tex} ...(i)
Put {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = v i.e. y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v + {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}
Equation (i) gives v + {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text v}{1+\text v^2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\text v^3}{1+\text v^2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1+\text v^2}{\text v^3} \text {dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{2 \text v^2}+\log |\text v|{/tex} = -log |x|+ log c
putting v = {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} and simplifying gives
{tex}-\frac{\text x^2}{2 \text y^2}{/tex} = log {tex}\left|\frac{\text c}{\text y}\right|{/tex}
now, x = 0, y = 1 gives c = 1
required solution is: {tex}\frac{\text x^2}{2 \text y^2}{/tex} = log |y|

28.(i)

Out of two bags, bag A contains 2 white and 3 red balls and bag B contains 4 white and 5 red balls. One ball is drawn at random from one of the bags and is found to be red. Find the probability that it was drawn from bag B.

Solution:

Let A, E1 and E2 denote the events that the ball is red,  chosen from bag A and chosen from bag B, respectively.
{tex}\therefore {/tex} P(E1) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
P(E2) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
Now,
{tex}P(\frac {A}{E_{1}})=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}P(\frac {A}{ E_{2}})=\frac{5}{9}{/tex}
Using Bayes' theorem, we get
Required probability = {tex}P(\frac {E_{2}}{ A})=\frac{P\left(E_{2}\right) P\left(\frac {A }{E_{2}}\right)}{P\left(E_{1}\right) P\left(\frac {A}{E_{1}}\right)+P\left(E_{2}\right) P\left(\frac {A }{E_{2}}\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{5}{9}}{\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{3}{5}+\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{5}{9}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{25}{52}{/tex}

33.(i)

Show that the lines {tex} \frac { x + 1 } { 3 } = \frac { y + 3 } { 5 } = \frac { z + 5 } { 7 }{/tex} and {tex} \frac { x - 2 } { 1 } = \frac { y - 4 } { 3 } = \frac { z - 6 } { 5 }{/tex} intersect. Also, find their point of intersection.

Solution:

The given lines are
{tex} \frac { x + 1 } { 3 } = \frac { y + 3 } { 5 } = \frac { z + 5 } { 7 } = \lambda{/tex}(let) ...(i)
and {tex} \frac { x - 2 } { 1 } = \frac { y - 4 } { 3 } = \frac { z - 6 } { 5 } = \mu ( \text { let } ){/tex} ...(ii)
Then, any point on line (i) is of the form
{tex} P ( 3 \lambda - 1,5 \lambda - 3,7 \lambda - 5 ){/tex} ...(iii)
and any point on line (ii) is of the form
{tex} Q ( \mu + 2,3 \mu + 4,5 \mu + 6 ){/tex} ...(iv)
If lines (i) and (ii) intersect, then these points must coincide for some {tex} \lambda{/tex} and {tex} \mu{/tex}.
Consider, {tex} 3 \lambda - 1 = \mu + 2{/tex}
{tex} 5 \lambda - 3 = 3 \mu + 4{/tex}
and {tex}7 \lambda - 5 = 5 \mu + 6{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 3 \mathrm { \lambda } - \mu = 3{/tex} ...(v)
{tex}5 \lambda - 3 \mu = 7{/tex} ...(vi)
and {tex}7 \lambda - 5 \mu = 11{/tex} ...(vii)
On multiplying Eq. (v) by 3 and then subtracting Eq. (vi), we get,
{tex} 9 \lambda - 3 u - 5 \lambda + 3 u = 9 - 7{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 4 \lambda = 2{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \lambda = \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex}
On putting the value of {tex} \lambda{/tex}  in Eq. (v), we get
{tex} 3 \times \frac { 1 } { 2 } - \mu = 3{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 3 } { 2 } - \mu = 3{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \mu = - \frac { 3 } { 2 }{/tex}
On putting the values of {tex} \lambda{/tex} and µ in Eq. (vii), we get
{tex} 7 \times \frac { 1 } { 2 } - 5 \left( - \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right) = 11{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 7 } { 2 } + \frac { 15 } { 2 } = 11 \Rightarrow \frac { 22 } { 2 } = 11{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} 11 = 11, which is true.
Hence, lines ( i) and (ii) intersect and their point of intersection is
{tex} P \left( 3 \times \frac { 1 } { 2 } - 1,5 \times \frac { 1 } { 2 } - 3,7 \times \frac { 1 } { 2 } - 5 \right){/tex}
[put {tex}\lambda = \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex} in Eq. (iii)]
i.e. {tex} \ P \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } , - \frac { 1 } { 2 } , - \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right){/tex}

In a group activity class, there are 10 students whose ages are 16, 17, 15, 14, 19, 17, 16, 19, 16 and 15 years. One student is selected at random such that each has equal chance of being chosen and age of the student is recorded.

  3. Use second derivative test to find the value of h for which cost of constructing the pool is minimum. What is the minimum cost of construction of the pool? (2)

  1. Capacity = area {tex}\times{/tex} depth = x2h = 250 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = {tex}\frac{250}{h}{/tex}
    C(cost ) = 500x2 + 4000h2
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = {tex}500\left(\frac{250}{h}\right){/tex} + 4000h2 = {tex}\frac{125000}{h}{/tex} + 4000h2
  2. {tex}\frac{\text {dC}}{\text {dh}}=-\frac{125000}{h^2}{/tex} + 8000h
    {tex}\frac{\text {dC}}{\text {dh}}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex} m or 2.5 m
  3. {tex}\frac{d^2 C}{d h^2}{/tex} = {tex}-125000\left(\frac{-2}{h^3}\right){/tex} + 8000 = {tex}\frac{250000}{h^3}{/tex} + 8000
    {tex}\left.\frac{\text d^2 \text C}{\text {dh}^2}\right|_{\text h = 2.5 \text m}{/tex} > 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cost is minimum when h = 2.5 m
    Minimum cost = C = {tex}\frac{125000}{\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)}+4000\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^2{/tex} = ₹ 75,000

OR

  1. we already have found above that h = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex} m when {tex}\frac{d C}{d h}{/tex} = 0
    for the values of h less than {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex} and close to {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{d C}{d h}{/tex} < 0
    and, for the values of h more than {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex} and close to {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{d C}{d h}{/tex} > 0
